steps.title: Recognize the area formula structure
steps.content: The area of a rectangle equals (length) × (width). We're given the formula $y = (49w)(w)$, where $y$ is the area. This means the product $(49w)(w)$ must represent length × width.

steps.title: Match the factors to the dimensions
steps.content: The problem states: length = 49 × (width). In our formula, $(49w) = 49 \times (w)$. Since length is 49 times the width, and $(49w)$ is 49 times $(w)$, the factor $(49w)$ must be the length and $(w)$ must be the width. Therefore, $49w$ represents the length of the rectangle in feet. That matches choice A — A is correct.
